string label for organization that is meaningful for human users

Identifier for the controlled vocabulary responsible for this keyword.
A representative code for this keyword in the controlled vocabulary (Optional).  Analogous to skos:Notation

1.description: identifier or name for the property concept quantified by the values in this variable slot. Multiple values can specify the property at different levels of granularity.

2.description: Catalog record metadata-about-metadata for this instrument description.
Schema or profile this metadata record conforms to.
Date this metadata record was last modified (ISO 8601).
Links to related resources (manuals, datasheets, calibration records, related instruments).
Sub-components of this instrument system. Each item is either an inline instrument description or an @id reference.
Component type classification.
Date the instrument was decommissioned (ISO 8601). Absent if still operational.
Date the instrument was commissioned (ISO 8601).
Instrument-specific properties (measured variables, detection limits, calibration information, operating parameters, etc.).
Agents associated with this instrument in specific roles (e.g. operator, custodian, principal investigator). Use schema:owner for the owning organization.
string label for person that is meaningful for human users
identifier for person, recommend ORCID
other labels by which the person might be known
if affiliation is present, value must be a schema:Organization.
restrict to email only. Schema.org allows telephone and postal contacts as well
other identifiers for the person
Organization that owns this instrument.
Instrument type classification(s) from a controlled vocabulary.
Product model information for this instrument.
Model identifier.
Model name or number.
Organization that manufactured this instrument.
Landing page URL for this instrument.
Formal identifier(s) for this instrument. Use PropertyValue for structured identifiers (e.g. PIDINST, serial number, inventory number).
In this context for the schema:PropertyValue, this field is an identifier for the identifier schema, e.g. DOI, ARK.  Get values from https://registry.identifiers.org/registry/ for interoperability
the identifier string. E.g. 10.5066/F7VX0DMQ
web-resolveable string for the identifier; host name part is location of a resolver that will return some representation for the given identifier value. E.g. https://doi.org/10.5066/F7VX0DMQ
Alternate name(s), e.g. specific make/model designation, common abbreviation.
Text description of this instrument, its purpose, and capabilities.
Human-readable name for this instrument.
Domain-specific type classifications. Should include a scientific instrument type URI (e.g. wd:Q3099911 for Wikidata scientific instrument).

properties.description: Human-readable name for the activity

Formal identifier for this activity
Primary responsible agent for this activity (maps to PROV wasAssociatedWith)
Other participants in this activity

Output entity produced by this activity (for action chaining, can be referenced as object of a subsequent activity)
Status of this activity
ISO8601 date-time when the activity started (maps to PROV startedAtTime)
ISO8601 date-time when the activity ended (maps to PROV endedAtTime)
Where the activity occurred
Methodology or protocol for this activity
Error description for failed activities
Domain-specific extension properties

identifier or name for the property concept quantified by the values in this variable slot. Multiple values can specify the property at different levels of granularity.
Domain-specific type classifications for this place (e.g. facility type, laboratory classification).
Formal identifier for this place (e.g. ROR identifier for a facility).
multiple place names or DefinedTerms that have a place name and URI for the location
extend base definition (AdditionalProperty) with some expected propertyID values from NEXUS and XDI specs added in the enum

Optional geographic extent using wkt geometry, see Ocean InfoHub (https://book.oceaninfohub.org/thematics/spatial/README.html#simple-geosparql-wkt). Other geometry schemes might be specified in a specific domain profile, e.g. for atmospheric, subsurface data, or local coordinate systems. NOTE that the location specified here should be the same as the schema.org point or contained within the specified bounding box.
Either a bounding box or a point location.

A GeoShape box defines a curvilinear geometry as a string of latitude-longitude coordinates. A space should be used to separate the latitude and longitude values. The coordinate point pairs are also separated by a space. 'East longitude' means positive longitude values are east of the prime (Greenwich) meridian. e.g. '39.33 120.77 40.44 123.96 41.00 121.34'

A GeoShape box defines an area on the surface of the earth defined by point locations of the southwest corner and northeast corner of the rectangle in latitude-longitude coordinates. A space should be used to separate the latitude and longitude values. The two corner coordinate points are separated by a space. 'East longitude' means positive longitude values are east of the prime (Greenwich) meridian.
Alternate or abbreviated name(s) for this place.
